what_it_does: A single-file CSS stylesheet for the Mockup Studio application UI, defining layout, component styling, interactive states, animations, and visual themes for the app chrome (wall, topbar, navigator spine/drawer, zone navigation, canvas), reusable components (cards, buttons, chips, forms, mockup thumbs), and assistant affordances (Ask Vi bar, orbital Vi, voice overlay).
observations: Warm plaster wall background built from layered radial gradients, an inline SVG frond texture, and a dotted noise overlay.
App grid uses a 72px icon rail plus canvas; a studio variant expands the left column to 328px for spine plus drawer.
Navigator is modeled as one continuous warm-paper surface: the spine and drawer share background, border, and shadow language.
Three switchable navigator join treatments (unfold, tab, bridge) are controlled by a data-nav-style attribute.
Zone/lifecycle navigation uses a gold 'hinge' and rail to connect the active spine zone to the unfolded drawer pages.
Project view includes a 5-stage lifecycle bar with progress fills and hover lift.
Build workspace has a crossing flow strip and a segmented pill toggle.
Card variants include default, quiet, ghost, and anti/rejected surfaces.
Vi assistant surfaces include a floating bottom ask bar, an orbital core with pulse rings, an expanded chat panel, and a full-screen voice overlay with orbiting rings and sparks.
Review styles reference MS_ARTIFACTS candidates, anchor/anti comparison, and focus cards.
Sequence workspace has a gold gradient spine rail with endpoint dots and a 7-column state grid.
Animations include shimmer, fade-in, drawer/spine entrances, and voice breathing; reduce-motion is handled via a data-reduce-motion attribute.
A legacy .rail section is retained but explicitly marked unused.
Most values reference external CSS custom properties (tokens) not defined in this file.
standouts: The three switchable navigator join treatments (unfold / tab / bridge) and the continuous spine+drawer concept.
The elaborate warm plaster wall background with frond SVG and noise texture.
The full-screen voice overlay with orbiting rings, sparks, and a breathing core.
The zone lifecycle redesign using a gold rail/hinge metaphor.
conventions: BEM-like naming with __ elements and -- modifiers.
Heavy reliance on CSS custom properties with var() references and fallback values.
Transform/opacity-biased animations with explicit reduce-motion support.
Design rationale written as long block comments.
Only one @media query for responsive behavior.
concerns: Large monolithic stylesheet mixing layout, chrome, components, states, and animations.
Many referenced CSS variables are not defined here, so token dependencies are opaque from this file alone.
Legacy .rail styles are retained though marked unused, increasing surface area.
Fixed/absolute positioned elements (ask-bar, orbit, vi-panel, voice-overlay) may need careful z-index coordination.
Reduce-motion support uses a custom data attribute rather than the standard prefers-reduced-motion media query.
notes: This is the warm, gold-accented application stylesheet that turns a token system into the Mockup Studio chrome, navigation, cards, and Vi assistant surfaces.
questions: Where are the ConceptV tokens (e.g. --surface-content, --r-xl, --ease-out, --status-success) defined?
What toggles the data-nav-style attribute among unfold/tab/bridge?
Is the legacy .rail block safe to remove, or kept for backward compatibility?
How are z-index layers coordinated between the wall, nav, topbar, ask-bar, orbit, vi-panel, and voice-overlay?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header states it inherits ConceptV tokens and the file is entirely application-level styling built on those token references.
apparent_themes: One continuous navigator surface rather than stacked panels.
Gold accent as connective tissue and active-state signal.
Quiet elevation through soft shadows and hairline borders.
Assistant-forward UI with Vi embedded in multiple surfaces.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a calm, premium creative-studio interface where navigation feels like unfolding a single sheet of warm paper, gold highlights guide attention across lifecycle zones, and an AI assistant is visually present throughout the app.
novelty: The conceptual model of spine+drawer as one continuous surface, plus three explicit switchable join treatments (unfold, seated tab, bridge), and a full-screen voice overlay with orbiting ring/spark animations.
contribution: A complete, opinionated application visual layer: the warm plaster wall canvas, the unified spine/drawer navigator with three join treatments, lifecycle/zone navigation language, reusable card/button/chip/form components, mockup/review/sequence-specific surfaces, and the Vi assistant affordances (ask bar, orbital core, voice overlay). It contributes a ready-to-use CSS vocabulary and interaction grammar for the studio chrome.
summary_for_embedding: Application-level CSS for the Mockup Studio, styling the warm plaster canvas, unified spine/drawer navigator with switchable join treatments, lifecycle zones, cards, buttons, forms, mockup/review/sequence surfaces, and the Vi assistant UI.
intention_for_embedding: To implement a calm, gold-accented creative-studio interface where navigation reads as one continuous warm-paper surface and AI assistance is visually embedded throughout.
